page: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is delivered via a 22 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute, memory, and compression workloads. The server achieves top-tier single-threaded performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2200.0 and a gzip compression speed of 34.2 GB/sec. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with a Passmark CPU score of 69316.76 and a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate of 340.83 GB/sec. Memory subsystem benchmarks show strong read-only bandwidth, reaching 3859.38 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 194.95 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate machine learning and cryptographic operations. This is reflected in strong LLM inference benchmarks, where the system processes 3555.38 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 254.3 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model. Given the combination of high-frequency compute, fast DDR5 memory, and a large local NVMe SSD, this instance is well-suited for compute-heavy workloads, database operations, software compilation, and real-time data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes a 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2200.0, and strong multi-core performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 69316.76. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) enables efficient execution of vector and matrix math. This server is optimized for compute-intensive applications, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD. It is powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor with AMX and AVX-512 support.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 for accelerated mathematical workloads.
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Strong performance in software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference.
